name: Ur
comment:
# Simple requests

@docs logIn, getShipName, scry, scryTask

values.name: getShipName
values.comment:
Queries the name of the current ship.
Return strings like `~zod` or `~racfer-hattes`.

    getShipName "http://localhost:8080"

values.name: logIn
values.comment:
Logs into your Urbit at the given root path and password.

    logIn "http://localhost:8080" "lidlut-tabwed-pillex-ridrup"

values.name: scry
values.comment:
Scry an agent at some path

    scry
        { url = "http://localhost:8080"
        , agent = "journal"
        , path = [ "entries", "all" ]
        , error = Noop
        , success =
            D.cell D.ignore
                (D.cell (D.const D.cord "jrnl")
                    (D.list (D.cell D.bigint D.cord |> D.map (\a b -> ( a, b ))))
                    |> D.map GotListings
                )
        }
values.type: { url : String.String, agent : Ur.Types.Agent, path : Ur.Types.Path, error : msg, success : Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor msg } -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: scryTask
values.comment: Same as `scry` but returns a `Task` instead of a `Cmd`.
values.type: { url : String.String, agent : Ur.Types.Agent, path : Ur.Types.Path, error : msg, success : Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor msg } -> Platform.Task a msg

name: Ur.Cmd
comment:
This module is conceptually similar to `Platform.Cmd`, but also allows you to send Urbit requests.

@docs Cmd, poke, cmd, none, batch

aliases.name: Cmd
aliases.comment: Like `Cmd` from `Platform.Cmd`, but for Urbit commands.

values.name: batch
values.comment: Batches multiple commands into one. Similar to `Platform.Cmd.batch`.

values.name: cmd
values.comment: Turns a `Platform.Cmd` command into a `Ur.Cmd` command.

values.name: none
values.comment: A command that does exactly nothing.

values.name: poke
values.comment:
Sends a %poke to a Gall Agent.

    poke
        { ship = "~zod"
        , agent = "journal"
        , mark = "journal-action"
        , noun = C.cell (C.cord "del") (C.bigint id)
        }
values.type: { ship : Ur.Types.Ship, agent : Ur.Types.Agent, mark : Ur.Types.Mark, noun : Ur.Types.Noun } -> Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg

name: Ur.Constructor

values.name: bigint
values.comment: Constructs an `Atom` from a `BigInt`.

values.name: bytes
values.comment: Constructs an `Atom` from `Bytes`.

values.name: cell
values.comment: Constructs a [Cell](https://developers.urbit.org/reference/glossary/cell) from two Nouns.

values.name: cord
values.comment: Constructs a [`cord`](https://developers.urbit.org/reference/glossary/cord), [`knot`](https://developers.urbit.org/guides/additional/strings#knot) or a [`term`](https://developers.urbit.org/guides/additional/strings#term) from a String.

values.name: float32
values.comment: Constructs an `Atom` from a 32-bit float.

values.name: float64
values.comment: Constructs an `Atom` from a 64-bit float.

values.name: int
values.comment:
Constructs an `Atom` from an unsigned 32-bit `Int`.
If you pass something other than an unsigned 32-bit integer the behaviour is undefined.

values.name: listOf
values.comment:
Constructs a `sig`-terminated list.

The first argument is a constructor for the elements of the list.

The second argument is the list of values to encode.

values.name: sig
values.comment:
The [`sig`](https://developers.urbit.org/reference/glossary/tape) value. Also known as `~`.

It is equivalent to `0`.

values.name: signedInt
values.comment: Constructs an `Atom` from a signed `Int`.

values.name: tape
values.comment: Constructs a [`tape`](https://developers.urbit.org/reference/glossary/tape) from a `String`.

values.name: posixToDa
values.comment: Converts `Posix` to a `@da`.

name: Ur.Deconstructor

values.name: bigint
values.comment: Extracts a `BigInt`.

values.name: bytes
values.comment: Extracts the raw `Byte`s of an `Atom`.

values.name: cell
values.comment:
Extracts a [`Cell`](https://developers.urbit.org/reference/glossary/cell) (pair) of two arbitrary values.

Accepts two arbitrary `Deconstructor`s that form a Cell.
values.type: Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor a -> Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor b -> Ur.Types.Noun -> Maybe.Maybe ( a, b )

values.name: const
values.comment:
Asserts that the value should be exactly equal to the second argument.

The first argument is a `Deconstructor` for the given type.

The second argument is the value to compare with.

This is useful to match on `term`s when there are multiple possible cases in a head-tagged union.

values.name: cord
values.comment: Extracts a `cord`.

values.name: debug
values.comment:
Debug a deconstructor.
If the deconstructor from the second argument fails then the failed nock will be printed to the console.
values.type: (String.String -> String.String) -> Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor a -> Ur.Types.Noun -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: float32
values.comment: Extracts a 32-bit `Float`.

values.name: float64
values.comment: Extracts a 64-bit `Float`.

values.name: ignore
values.comment:
Ignore any value.

This is useful when you have a value you don't care about. `ignore` allows you to just skip the value.

values.name: int
values.comment:
Extracts a 32-bit unsigned `Int`.

If the `Atom` is larger than 32 bits the the behaviour is undefined.

values.name: lazy
values.comment:
Maps (applies) a function to all of the values deconstructed.

This is useful when you want to create a data type with extracted values as fields.

values.name: list
values.comment:
Extracts a sig-terminated list of arbitrary elements.

The first argument is a `Deconstructor` of the elements of the list.

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps over the result of the deconstructor.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor a -> Ur.Types.Noun -> Maybe.Maybe b

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Try to execute all of the `Deconstructor`s in order until one succeeds.

This is especially useful for deconstructing head-tagged unions from Hoon.

values.name: run
values.comment: Executes a `Deconstructor` on a `Noun`.

values.name: runBytes
values.comment: Executes a deconstructor on a `jam`med `Noun`.

values.name: sig
values.comment: Asserts the the `Atom` should be exactly `sig` (`~`).

values.name: signedInt
values.comment:
Extracts a 32-bit signed `Int`.

If the `Atom` is larger than 32 bits the the behaviour is undefined.

values.name: tape
values.comment: Extracts a `tape`.

values.name: tar
values.comment:
Extract the raw `Noun`.

Always succeeds.

values.name: fromString
values.comment: Converts a ship name like `~zod` into an `Atom`.

name: Ur.Run
comment:
This module contains functions to run your application with Urbit integration.

@docs element, document, application, Program, Model, Msg

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: An Urbit wrapper around your application messages.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment: An Urbit wrapper around your application model.
aliases.type: { subscriptions : Ur.Run.SubDict msg, subscriptionIntMapping : Dict.Dict Basics.Int ( String.String, String.String, List.List String.String ), app : app, connected : Basics.Bool, eventId : Basics.Int, flags : Ur.Run.Flags, requestsToRetry : List.List Ur.Requests.UrbitRequest, sinks : Dict.Dict Basics.Int Ur.Types.Noun }

values.name: application
values.comment: The same as `Browser.element` but with urbit stuff added around it.
values.type: { init : Url.Url -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), view : model -> Browser.Document msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, urbitSubscriptions : model -> Ur.Sub.Sub msg, onUrlRequest : Browser.UrlRequest -> msg, onUrlChange : Url.Url -> msg, createEventSource : String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Ur.Run.Msg msg), onEventSourceMsg : (Json.Decode.Value -> Ur.Run.Msg msg) -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Ur.Run.Msg msg), urbitUrl : model -> String.String } -> Ur.Run.Program model msg

values.name: document
values.comment: The same as `Browser.element` but with urbit stuff added around it.
values.type: { init : ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), view : model -> Browser.Document msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, urbitSubscriptions : model -> Ur.Sub.Sub msg, createEventSource : String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Ur.Run.Msg msg), onEventSourceMsg : (Json.Decode.Value -> Ur.Run.Msg msg) -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Ur.Run.Msg msg), urbitUrl : model -> String.String } -> Ur.Run.Program model msg

values.name: element
values.comment: The same as `Browser.element` but with urbit stuff added around it.
values.type: { init : ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), view : model -> Html.Html msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Ur.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, urbitSubscriptions : model -> Ur.Sub.Sub msg, createEventSource : String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Ur.Run.Msg msg), onEventSourceMsg : (Json.Decode.Value -> Ur.Run.Msg msg) -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Ur.Run.Msg msg), urbitUrl : model -> String.String } -> Ur.Run.Program model msg

name: Ur.Sub
comment:
This module is conceptually similar to `Platform.Sub`, but allows you to subscribe to Urbit channels.

@docs Sub, subscribe, none, batch, sink

aliases.name: Sub
aliases.comment: Like `Sub` from `Platform.Sub` but for Urbit subscriptions.

values.name: batch
values.comment: Batch multiple subscriptions into one.

values.name: none
values.comment: A subscription that does exactly nothing. (Does not subscribe to anything)

values.name: sink
values.comment: Creates a %sink subscription.
values.type: { ship : String.String, app : String.String, path : List.List String.String, deconstructor : Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor msg } -> Ur.Sub.Sub msg

values.name: subscribe
values.comment:
Creates an Urbit subscription.

    subscribe
        { ship = ship
        , app = "journal"
        , path = [ "updates" ]
        , deconstructor = decodeJournalUpdate |> D.map GotUpdate
        }
values.type: { ship : String.String, app : String.String, path : List.List String.String, deconstructor : Ur.Deconstructor.Deconstructor msg } -> Ur.Sub.Sub msg

name: Ur.Types

aliases.name: Ship
aliases.comment:
A ship name like `~zod` or `~racfer-hattes`.

Also know as `@p`.

name: Ur.Uw
comment:
This module works with Urbit base-64 encoded strings aka `@uw`.

@docs decode, encode
